description_long: This object is a vintage reel-to-reel tape recorder and radio receiver, featuring a brushed metal front panel with two large tape reels, multiple dials, switches, and analog meters. The front face is dominated by the tape mechanism, with a set of control knobs and switches for playback, recording, and tuning, as well as a frequency display and VU meters. The sides and top are encased in a wood veneer, giving it a classic, retro appearance. The back panel includes a large ventilation fan, various input/output ports, and a label with technical information. The device sits on four rubber feet, elevating it slightly above the surface. The overall design is boxy and robust, typical of mid-20th-century audio equipment.
description: A vintage reel-to-reel tape recorder and radio receiver with a wood veneer casing, metal front panel, and multiple control dials and meters.
description_view_0: The front view shows the metal control panel with two tape reels, several knobs, switches, and analog meters, as well as the frequency display.
description_view_1: The left side view displays the wood veneer casing with a visible hinge and a vent on the top, with no controls or features.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals a large ventilation fan, technical labels, input/output ports, and four rubber feet for stability.
description_view_3: The right side view shows the wood veneer casing, a hinge, and a slot, with minimal visible controls.
